description: Canadian outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1965 in Oshawa, Ontario. It was the second largest outlaw motorcycle club in the world by 1970 with over 400 members. The club was involved in the first major outlaw biker conflict in Canadian history against the Popeyes from 1974 to 1976. It disbanded in 2000 when most chapters joined the Hells Angels, was briefly reactivated in 2017 and again in 2025.

country_links.evidence_quote: 34 chapters in Canada 4 in USA and 2 in Finland
country_links.context: The organization has chapters in Finland.

country_links.evidence_quote: William 'Gatemouth' Edson of the Outlaw chapter in Fort Lauderdale, Florida had been convicted of multiple offenses... Edson had escaped from prison in Florida and with the help of his fellow Outlaws had made his way to Canada, where he settled in Kitchener.
country_links.context: William 'Gatemouth' Edson of the Outlaw chapter in Fort Lauderdale, Florida had been convicted of multiple offenses... Edson had escaped from prison in Florida and with the help of his fellow Outlaws had made his way to Canada, where he settled in Kitchener.
